item.title: UK joins global push to rein in children's screen use with national guidance
item.description: Britain has told parents to curb young children's screen time, advising no screens for under-2s and up to an hour a day ​for 2- to 5-year-olds because prolonged solo use can disrupt sleep and ‌displace play and exercise.

item.title: Morning Bid: You can't handle the 'Truth'
item.description: Everything Mike Dolan and the ROI team are excited to read, watch and listen to over the weekend.

item.title: US equity funds rebound with strongest inflows in four months
item.description: U.S. equity funds drew strong inflows in the week to March 25 as hopes for de-escalation in the Middle ​East lifted sentiment after U.S. President Donald Trump postponed an ‌attack on Iranian energy infrastructure and proposed a deal to end the war.

item.title: US stock futures subdued as Iran strike delay offers limited relief
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were muted on Friday even as investors assessed prospects ‌of de-escalation in the Middle East after another delayed deadline for U.S. strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: SoftBank secures $40 billion loan to fund further OpenAI investment
item.description: SoftBank Group ​said on Friday ‌it has secured a $40 billion loan through ​a bridge facility ​to fund further investments ⁠in ChatGPT maker ​OpenAI and for general corporate ​purposes.

item.title: US corn planting seen down, soy acres up as Iran war inflates costs, analysts say
item.description: The Iran war has upended the planting intentions of U.S. farmers, resulting in fewer acres of corn and the lowest quantity of spring wheat planted since 1970 as rising fertilizer and fuel costs and low grain prices dim the outlook ​for profits, analysts said ahead of a U.S. government report due on Tuesday.

item.title: Wall St Week Ahead US jobs data to give economic view for war-gripped markets
item.description: Next week's U.S. employment report headlines a fresh batch of economic data for stock investors, who also will closely follow developments in an Iran war that is entering its second month.

item.title: Global equity funds see biggest inflows in 2-1/2 months on Iran de-escalation hopes
item.description: Global equity funds attracted their biggest weekly inflow in nearly 2-1/2 months in the week through March 25 after U.S. President Donald Trump delayed strikes ​on Iranian energy infrastructure, raising hopes of a temporary de-escalation and ‌easing oil shock fears.

item.title: Indonesia's social media curbs for kids set for Saturday, but few know how it will work
item.description: Armed with a smartphone, Anza Zafran Utama, a 9-year-old boy in the Indonesian city of Bogor, is either a dinosaur or a shooter, depending on ​his mood.

item.title: VW's software partnership with Rivian clears investment hurdle
item.description: Volkswagen's software partnership with Rivian , a ​cornerstone of CEO Oliver Blume's turnaround strategy for ‌the German auto group, has passed a key milestone, bringing the U.S. partner closer to its next ​tranche of funding.

item.title: China launches two probes into US trade practices
item.description: China's commerce ministry has initiated two counter-probes into U.S. practices ​that hamper the flow of Chinese products into ‌the United States, it said on Friday, refraining from immediate retaliation to U.S. measures announced earlier this month.

item.title: Nearly half a million customers hit by Lloyds IT glitch that exposed transaction data, committee says
item.description: Lloyds Banking Group exposed the personal data of up to 447,936 customers during an ​IT glitch earlier this month that ‌allowed users to see other customers' transactions, including account details and national insurance numbers, Britain's Treasury ​Committee said on Friday.

item.title: Pernod-Ricard shares pare losses as market digests merger talks
item.description: Shares in French spirits maker Pernod ​Ricard clawed back some losses on Friday after ‌slumping in the previous session when it confirmed talks on a possible tie-up with Jack Daniel's owner Brown-Forman .

item.title: Italy investigates Sephora and Benefit Cosmetics over marketing to children
item.description: The Italian competition authority AGCM has launched two investigations into ​LVMH-owned Sephora and Benefit Cosmetics over possible ‌unfair commercial practices linked to the marketing of products for adults to children and adolescents, it said on ​Friday.

item.title: Japan's ispace delays NASA-sponsored moon landing to 2030
item.description: Japanese spacecraft startup ispace said on Friday it will further delay a U.S. government-sponsored lunar mission to 2030 and cut its global workforce, in a strategic shift ​after two failed lunar landings.

item.title: With 'no place to hide' traders spend sleepless nights as Iran war roils markets
item.description: For Wang Yapei, it's all about sleeping well at night. The Shanghai-based fund manager has cut positions aggressively in the face of a steep selloff that has torn through global markets as the war in the Middle East rages on.

item.title: Australia court fines Binance Australia $6.9 million over client onboarding failures
item.description: The Australian federal ​court has ‌ordered local derivatives ​business ​of cryptocurrency exchange Binance ⁠to ​pay an ​A$10 million ($6.9 million) penalty for ​misclassifying ​over 85% of ‌its ⁠Australian client base, the country's ​securities ​regulator ⁠said on ​Friday.

item.title: China boycotts top AI conference after ban on papers from US-sanctioned entities
item.description: China's largest federation for science and technology professionals on Friday announced a boycott of a top artificial intelligence conference after the California-based foundation that ​runs the gathering stopped accepting submissions from entities under U.S. sanctions.

item.title: Exclusive: Huawei's new AI chip find favour with ByteDance, Alibaba which plan to place orders, sources say
item.description: Customer testing of Huawei's new AI chip, designed to challenge Nvidia in the China market, ‌has gone well and big tech giants including ByteDance and Alibaba plan to place orders, two people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Novartis to buy U.S.-based biotech firm Excellergy for up to $2 billion
item.description: Swiss pharma company Novartis said on Friday ​it will acquire California-based ‌biotech company Excellergy in a deal worth up ​to $2 billion.

item.title: Chinese universities with military links bought Super Micro servers with restricted AI chips
item.description: Four Chinese ​universities, including two linked to the People's Liberation Army, bought Super Micro Computer servers with restricted AI chips over the past year, ‌procurement data shows, even as the U.S. clamps down on sales of some advanced processors to China.

item.title: Morning Bid: For hungry markets, it was a kids' menu TACO
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Stella Qiu

item.title: Asian stocks extend global rout; bonds hammered as war drags on
item.description: Asian stock markets were swept up in a global ​rout on Friday, tracking Wall Street lower as the threat of a protracted energy shock out of the war-torn Middle East ‌sent borrowing costs spiralling higher.

item.title: China detaining Panama-flagged ships amid battle over port control, FMC says
item.description: The U.S. Federal Maritime Commission (FMC) said on Thursday it ‌is closely monitoring a surge in detentions of Panama-flagged vessels in China that appears tied to a Panama court ruling against Hong Kong-based CK Hutchison.

item.title: China banks outperform broader market after news of shareholding rule changes
item.description: China's banking stocks outperformed the broader market on Friday after news that ​Beijing is considering easing shareholder restrictions ‌to broaden capital-raising options for lenders.

item.title: Dollar rides haven demand as Middle East talks ring hollow
item.description: The dollar pushed higher toward multi-month peaks on Friday ​as investors sought safety in the shadow of an intensifying Middle East war and mounting doubts ‌over any path to de-escalation.

item.title: Oil prices fall as Trump pauses attacks on Iranian energy plants
item.description: Oil prices fell in early trade on Friday and were down over a volatile week after U.S. President Donald Trump said talks with Iran to end the war were going "very well" ​and announced he would pause attacks on the country's energy plants for 10 days.

item.title: White House AI czar Sacks to step down, moves to advisory role
item.description: White House artificial intelligence and crypto czar David Sacks is stepping down from his role, saying in ​an interview on Bloomberg Television on Thursday he ‌had reached the end of his time as a special government employee.

item.title: Exclusive: China's top chipmaker has supplied chipmaking tech to Iran military, US officials say
item.description: SMIC, China's largest chipmaker, has sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, two senior Trump ‌administration officials said on Thursday, raising questions about Beijing's stance in the month-old U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran.

item.title: Ousted Ben & Jerry's board chair sues Unilever, alleging defamation
item.description: Unilever and its ​recently spun-off Magnum ice cream unit were sued for defamation on Thursday by Anuradha Mittal, ‌who was ousted in December as chair of Ben & Jerry's independent board, saying the companies vilified and discredited her for supporting Palestinian rights.
